prerequisites: A running Yellowbrick instance (version 7 or greater) reachable from Airbyte. Airbyte Cloud requires SSL or TLS encryption when connecting.
A Yellowbrick database to load data into. Create one with UTF-8 encoding, for example: CREATE DATABASE airbyte_test ENCODING=UTF8;
A Yellowbrick user configured to enable the JSON type and functions, with permission to create a database. Verify JSON support with SHOW enable_full_json;
The connection coordinates: host, port (defaults to 5432), database name, target schema, and a username/password for the Yellowbrick user.
Network access from Airbyte to the Yellowbrick host. The host address is available from Yellowbrick Manager.

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Yellowbrick database user via the username and password fields. The password is stored as a secret. This is the standard authentication for the connection.

authMethods.name: SSL / TLS encryption (ssl_mode)
authMethods.summary: Set ssl and ssl_mode to control connection encryption. Modes range from disable, allow, prefer, and require to verify-ca and verify-full. The verification modes accept secret certificate material via ssl_mode.ca_certificate, ssl_mode.client_certificate, ssl_mode.client_key, and ssl_mode.client_key_password. Airbyte Cloud only supports connecting with SSL or TLS encryption.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel (tunnel_method)
authMethods.summary: Optionally reach Yellowbrick through an SSH bastion. Choose No Tunnel, SSH Key Authentication (provide the private key via tunnel_method.ssh_key), or Password Authentication (provide tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password). Both tunnel secrets are stored securely.

setupSteps.title: Prepare the Yellowbrick database and user
setupSteps.body: Create the destination database with UTF-8 encoding (for example CREATE DATABASE airbyte_test ENCODING=UTF8;) and ensure the Yellowbrick user has JSON type/functions enabled and permission to create a database. Confirm JSON support with SHOW enable_full_json;.

setupSteps.title: Enter connection details
setupSteps.body: Provide the required fields: host (your Yellowbrick instance address from Yellowbrick Manager), port (default 5432), database, schema (the default schema, or several comma-separated schemas, set in the search path), and username.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter the password for the Yellowbrick user. Optionally set jdbc_url_params for advanced JDBC connection options such as connectTimeout (defaults to 60 seconds).

setupSteps.title: Configure encryption
setupSteps.body: Enable ssl and select an ssl_mode. For verify-ca or verify-full, supply ssl_mode.ca_certificate and, where mutual TLS is used, ssl_mode.client_certificate, ssl_mode.client_key, and ssl_mode.client_key_password. SSL or TLS is required on Airbyte Cloud.

setupSteps.title: Optionally configure an SSH tunnel
setupSteps.body: If Yellowbrick is not directly reachable, set tunnel_method to SSH Key Authentication (provide tunnel_method.ssh_key) or Password Authentication (provide tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password). Otherwise leave it as No Tunnel.

sources.title: Connect Airbyte | Yellowbrick Data Product Documentation
